sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! After clicking purple at its new position:
- White moved from (38-42, 31-35) to (47-51, 28-32) - jumped to purple's old position
- Purple moved from (47-51, 28-32) to (52-56, 26-30) - pushed down toward gray
- Gray is at (57-61, 25-29)
- Timer: 60 pixels
So the pattern is clear: clicking purple causes the diamond above it (white) to jump to purple's position, and purple gets pushed down toward the next diamond.
Now purple is at (52-56, 26-30) and gray is at (57-61, 25-29). They're very close! Purple's bottom is at row 56, and gray's top is at row 57. They're almost adjacent.
If I click purple again, white should jump to purple's position (52-56, 26-30), and purple should get pushed down to where gray is (57-61, 25-29). This might cause purple and gray to merge or interact.
Let me click purple one more time to see what happens when purple reaches gray.
Purple is now at (52-56, 26-30), so I should click around (54, 28).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Purple at (52-56, 26-30), click to push into gray
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 28}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== After pushing purple into gray ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['M', 'W', 'p', 'G'] and n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [b[0] for b in n['boundary']]
        cols = [b[1] for b in n['boundary']]
        print(f"{n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print(f"\nStep: {current_frame.step}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over', False)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
